data.title: Elliott Investment Management exits Cardinal Health, cuts NRG Energy, Southwest Air
data.description:
Famed activist investor Elliott Investment Management exited its position in Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH), dumping 2M shares in Q4, according to its 13F filing.

The hedge fund increased its stake in CorMedix (CRMD) by 1.3M shares and added to its stake in HDFC Bank (HDB), according to the filing on Friday.

Elliott decreased its position in NRG Energy (NRG) shedding 4.9M shares and its position in Southwest Air (LUV), trimming 1.45M shares, according to the 13F filing.

data.title: Codexis announces the approval of equity grants for new employees
data.description:
• Codexis (NASDAQ:CDXS) announced the approval of equity grants to five new employees as approved by the compensation committee of Codexis’ boards.
  • The newly hired employees received equity awards consisting of an aggregate of options to purchase 39,000 shares of Codexis common stock and 27,500 restricted stock units as inducement awards under the company’s 2024 inducement plan.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Outcrop Silver & Gold announces director appointments
data.description:
• Outcrop Silver & Gold (OCG:CA) announced that Ian Harris, chief executive; Jay Sujir, chair; Kevin Nishi, chair of the audit committee; Joseph Hebert, and Ana Milena Vásquez were elected as directors of Outcrop Silver.
  • Ian Slater did not stand for re-election and is no longer a director, effective today. 
  • Slater will continue to support Outcrop Silver as a special advisor.
  • Source : Press release

data.title: Banxa Holdings receives early warning report on acquisition of 763,600 shares by Antanas Guoga
data.description:
• Antanas Guoga filed an early warning report regarding the acquisition of 763,600 common shares in the capital of Banxa Holdings (TSXV:BNXA:CA) ("Banxa") on February 14, 2025.
  • Prior to the completion of the acquisition, Guoga held an aggregate of 4.57M shares, representing ~10.02% shares.
  • Upon completion of the acquisition, Guoga held an aggregate of 5.33M shares, representing ~11.69% shares.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: SoftBank adds to shares in VTEX, YMM, cuts NMRA and exits DASH, among other moves
data.description:
The Japanese investment holding company SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) purchased two new stakes: 20.45M shares in Pacific Biosciences of California (PACB), and 14.66M shares in Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX), according to the company’s latest 13F filings.

The bank also increased its stake in software-as-a-service digital commerce platform VTEX (VTEX) by 93.4% from 19.8M in the third quarter to 38.4M shares in the fourth quarter.

In addition, SoftBank increased its stake in Full Truck Alliance (YMM) by almost 280%, from 1.1M shares in the third quarter to 4.2M shares in the fourth quarter.

SoftBank reduced its stake in Neumora Therapeutics (NMRA) by 13.3% from 7.5M shares in the third quarter to 6.5M shares in the fourth quarter.

Other cuts included stakes in Lemonade (LMND) – cutting by 10.7% to 10.7M shares in the fourth quarter vs. the third quarter 2024 – and Nu Holdings (NU) by about 10% to 21M shares.

Lastly, the bank exited stakes in two companies, selling 1.6M shares of IonQ Inc. (IONQ) and 5.6M shares of Exscientia Plc. The bank also exited its 23.37K shares stake in DoorDash (DASH).

More on 13F filings:
  • Coatue adds Reddit, ServiceNow stakes, exits Chipotle, FXI, among top Q4 trades
  • Saudi Arabia sovereign fund exits Walmart, buys DoorDash, doubles Amazon among Q4 moves
  • Soros Capital takes new Nvidia, Chipotle stakes, exits Alibaba, Microsoft, among top Q4 moves
  • SoundHound AI, Serve, Nano-X in focus as Nvidia exits stakes; cuts Arm investment
  • Ray Dalio's Bridgewater takes new stake in Tesla, exits Micron & Goldman Sachs in Q4 moves

data.description:

  • Filing

data.title: Pershing Square cuts Seapoint Entertainment stake by 16.6% to 4.2M shares in Q4
Jana Partners adds Markel Group, exits Fortrea
data.description:
Jana Partners added Markel Group (NYSE:MKL) in Q4, purchasing 109K shares, according to its latest 13F filing.

The activist hedge fund exited Fortrea Holdings (NASDAQ:FTRE) and Frontier Communications (FYBR), which agreed to be sold to Verizon (VZ) in September, according to the 13F filing.

Jana increased its position in Lamb Weston (LW), adding 3.7M shares, and Rapid7 (RPD).

The hedge fund decreased its stakes in Fidelity National (FIS), Wolfspeed (WOLF), cutting 1.18M shares, and Trimble (TRMB), according to the 13F filing.
